svg.title: Healthcare data platform — VPC network architecture
svg.desc: Structural diagram of the AWS VPC showing 3 public subnets with NAT gateways and an internet gateway, 3 private subnets with Lambda, Glue, and Redis, and security group rules between them across us-east-1a, 1b, and 1c.
svg.text: Hospital SFTP clients · AWS service public endpoints (S3, SQS, DynamoDB, KMS)
public subnets — NAT gateways only live here
private subnets — Lambda · Glue · Redis · no public IPs · inbound from internet impossible
prod: 3 NAT gateways (one per AZ) · non-prod: 1 NAT gateway (cost saving) · controlled by
outputs → vpc_id · private_subnet_ids[3] · public_subnet_ids[3] · glue/lambda/redis sg_ids
